target.title: fix(cron): persist resolved delivery target into isolated cron session deliveryContext
target.body:
## Summary

Isolated cron completion delivery can fail with `Channel is required (no configured channels detected)` even when the job explicitly sets `delivery.channel`, because the resolved delivery target is never written back to the isolated cron session entry's `deliveryContext`. Completion-announce paths such as `sessions_send`/`subagent_announce` later resolve the target from the session entry and see an empty context.

This change persists the resolved cron delivery target onto the isolated cron session entry right after `resolveCronDeliveryContext`, so explicit `delivery.channel` survives to the final completion announcer.

Fixes #92460.

## Changes

- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-session-state.ts`: add `setCronSessionDeliveryContextFromResolvedDelivery`, a focused helper that normalizes and writes a resolved delivery target into the session entry's `deliveryContext` only when resolution succeeds and a routable target exists.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`: call the helper in `prepareCronRunContext` after `resolveCronDeliveryContext` returns.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-session-state.test.ts`: add regression tests covering explicit target persistence, missing optional fields, failed-resolution no-op, and empty-target no-op.
- `scripts/repro/issue-92460-cron-completion-delivery-context.mts`: standalone reproduction script that exercises the production helper and verifies the explicit channel/to/account/thread survive.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: explicit `delivery.channel` on an isolated cron job is now persisted onto the isolated session entry, so completion-announce target resolution no longer falls back to a channel-less main session.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v22.19.0, local OpenClaw source checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-92460-cron-completion-delivery-context.mts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  Terminal output from running the standalone reproduction script against the patched source:
  ```text
  === Reproduction for issue #92460 ===
  Isolated cron session deliveryContext: {
    \"channel\": \"webchat\",
    \"to\": \"controller\",
    \"accountId\": \"default\",
    \"threadId\": \"thread-42\"
  }
  PASS: explicit delivery.channel survives to the session entry deliveryContext
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: the isolated cron session entry now carries a normalized deliveryContext with the explicit channel, to, account, and thread from the resolved delivery target. Before the patch the same script would leave deliveryContext empty and the entry would lose the explicit webchat target.
- **What was not tested**: live channel send integration (no configured webchat gateway in the test environment); only the session-state propagation path and existing cron delivery-target resolution tests were exercised.

## Verification

- [x] `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-92460-cron-completion-delivery-context.mts` passes
- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run-session-state.test.ts --run` passes
- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts --run` passes
- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts --run` passes
- [x] `npx oxlint` on changed files passes
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt` on changed files passes
- [x] `pnpm build` passes
- [x] `pnpm tsgo` passes

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix — Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix: carry reply metadata into runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a first-class current-turn reply metadata payload to `CurrentInboundPromptContext` so Telegram/forum reply facts survive the reply prompt/runtime handoff as structured OpenClaw metadata.

This is the Morty patch shape, polished for upstream:

- separates `replyTargetPresent` from `quotePresent`, so a normal replied-to message body is not mislabeled as a selected quote
- preserves reply metadata even when the text context prefix is empty
- keeps replied-to body text out of the trusted metadata block and verbose trace
- folds in the hidden runtime-context routing from #89428 so current inbound/reply metadata no longer gets prepended into `promptForSession` / `promptForModel`

## Related

Addresses the runtime handoff portion of #88032.

Builds on the same routing direction as #89428. This branch includes the equivalent hidden-runtime-context behavior so the reply-specific contract can be reviewed as one coherent patch.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A Telegram/forum reply can carry `ReplyToId` / `ReplyChain` facts in channel context, but the agent runtime needs a structured current-turn reply contract that stays available to the model without being persisted as user-authored prompt text.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch on Linux, running the real TypeScript modules through `node --import tsx`. The proof uses redacted Telegram/forum-style ids.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** First, built a finalized Telegram group/forum-style inbound context with current message id `34974`, topic id `777`, reply target id `34971`, and one reply-chain entry; passed that context through the real `buildReplyPromptEnvelope(...)` prompt-prelude path; rendered the current-turn runtime prefix with `buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(...)`; checked that structured reply metadata appears before untrusted context and that the trusted reply metadata block does not include the replied-to body text.

```bash
PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node --import tsx -e 'const { finalizeInboundContext } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-context.ts"); const { buildReplyPromptEnvelope } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.ts"); const { buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix } = await import("./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts"); const sessionCtx = finalizeInboundContext({ Body: "Sean, answer this old reply", BodyStripped: "Sean, answer this old reply", Provider: "telegram", Surface: "telegram", OriginatingChannel: "telegram", ChatType: "group", MessageSid: "34974", MessageThreadId: 777, ReplyToId: "34971", ReplyToBody: "old target body", ReplyToSender: "Alice", ReplyChain: [{ messageId: "34971", threadId: "777", sender: "Alice", body: "old target body" }] }); const envelope = buildReplyPromptEnvelope({ ctx: sessionCtx, sessionCtx, baseBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", prefixedBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", hasUserBody: true, inboundUserContext: "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971", isBareSessionReset: false, startupAction: "new" }); const prefix = buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(envelope.currentInboundContext); console.log(JSON.stringify({ reply: envelope.currentInboundContext?.reply, text: envelope.currentInboundContext?.text, metadataBeforeUntrusted: prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") >= 0 && prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") < prefix.indexOf("Conversation info"), metadataLeaksReplyBody: prefix.slice(0, prefix.indexOf("Conversation info")).includes("old target body") }, null, 2));'
```

Then, exercised the hidden runtime-context routing with the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper and verified that reply metadata stays out of the submitted/persisted prompt strings while remaining in `runtimeContext`.

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'PROOF'
import { resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts';
const parts = resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts({
  effectivePrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  transcriptPrompt: 'what is this referring to?',
  currentInboundContext: {
    text: 'Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=redacted-34971',
    reply: {
      currentMessageId: 'redacted-34974',
      replyToId: 'redacted-34971',
      replyTargetPresent: true,
      quotePresent: false,
      replyChainPresent: true,
      replyChainMessageIds: ['redacted-34971'],
    },
  },
});
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  prompt: parts.prompt,
  modelPrompt: parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt,
  promptContainsReplyMetadata: parts.prompt.includes('Current reply metadata') || parts.prompt.includes('redacted-34971'),
  modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata: (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('Current reply metadata') || (parts.modelPrompt ?? parts.prompt).includes('redacted-34971'),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('Current reply metadata')),
  runtimeContextContainsReplyToId: Boolean(parts.runtimeContext?.includes('redacted-34971')),
}, null, 2));
PROOF
```

**Evidence after fix:** Console output from the first real module command:

```json
{
  "reply": {
    "currentMessageId": "34974",
    "threadId": "777",
    "replyToId": "34971",
    "replyTargetPresent": true,
    "quotePresent": false,
    "replyChainPresent": true,
    "replyChainMessageIds": ["34971"]
  },
  "text": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971",
  "metadataBeforeUntrusted": true,
  "metadataLeaksReplyBody": false
}
```

Console output from the hidden runtime-context proof:

```json
{
  "prompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "modelPrompt": "what is this referring to?",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Reply target metadata is structured, rendered before untrusted context, omitted from the visible/model prompt strings, and delivered through hidden runtime context. The trusted reply metadata block does not leak the replied-to body text.


**Live Telegram/forum transport proof (redacted):** A real Telegram forum reply was sent in an existing group topic on 2026-06-05 UTC. No live OpenClaw install or runtime was changed for this transport proof.

Redacted inbound metadata supplied to the agent turn:

```json
{
  "channel": "telegram",
  "surface": "telegram",
  "chat_type": "group",
  "is_forum": true,
  "topic_id": "<redacted-topic>",
  "message_id": "<redacted-current>",
  "reply_to_id": "<redacted-anchor>",
  "was_mentioned": true,
  "has_reply_context": true
}
```

The assembled current message included a Telegram reply target for the proof anchor while the persisted session user message remained the user-authored text only:

```json
{
  "role": "user",
  "sourceChannel": "telegram",
  "content": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745"
}
```

Then the PR branch was exercised with the same redacted Telegram/forum shape through the real `resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts(...)` helper:

```json
{
  "prompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "modelPrompt": "@crustaSean_bot PROOF_REPLY_90745",
  "promptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "modelPromptContainsReplyMetadata": false,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyMetadata": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsReplyToId": true,
  "runtimeContextContainsForumSignal": true
}
```

This pairs a real Telegram/forum reply event with the PR-branch runtime handoff: the transport supplies `reply_to_id`/forum/mention facts, the session prompt remains clean user text, and the PR branch keeps reply/forum metadata in hidden runtime context instead of visible/model prompt text.

**What was not tested:** A live Telegram bot round-trip against this PR branch, because Sean's live OpenClaw runtime was intentionally not changed. The local checkout does not have `rastermill` hydrated, so the broader embedded-attempt context-engine shard currently fails during media imports before reaching these assertions; I did not run dependency install as part of this PR polish.

## Tests

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts` - passed 2 Vitest shards, 36 tests
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- attempted `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` - reply/runtime shards passed, embedded-attempt shard was blocked by missing local package `rastermill`

target.title: Polish Workboard operations view
target.body:
## Summary
- polish the Workboard operations UI across card layout, filters, health metrics, details, responsive behavior, dropdowns, and tooltips
- add guarded auto-refresh, serialized writes, linked-task reconciliation, bounded task discovery, and safer start/stop behavior
- preserve accessible names, selected custom-select values, tooltip viewport behavior, and native-title fallback semantics
- expand focused controller/view/tooltip/lifecycle coverage and deterministic Workboard browser E2E

## Maintenance repairs
- rebased onto current Workboard base behavior while preserving main's hide-empty-column and accessibility changes
- deferred failed lifecycle refresh retries, preserved matching task links on enrichment failure, and committed poll results atomically so in-flight polls cannot disrupt drag-and-drop
- hardened canonical/replacement task matching, terminal discovery cursor cycling, unresolved-start tracking, and task/session stop fallback behavior
- removed an unreachable same-card details toggle while keeping the modal/inert interaction model intact
- hardened shared floating tooltip cleanup, viewport placement, accessible descriptions, and inherited native-title suppression
- made custom-select triggers announce both their field and selected value

## Verification
Proof was run on exact pushed head `8d8b4680a8611887f84a2a6d0c82e291cf687d1e`.

- `git merge-tree --write-tree HEAD upstream/main` - clean against current `upstream/main` `c9c19a1106699bb633b74dd67b89cb771cf4069e`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- focused UI suite - 7 files, 261 tests passed
- complete UI shard - 113 files, 1,525 tests passed
- Workboard browser E2E - 2 files, 2 tests passed
- `pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- scoped `oxfmt --check` and `oxlint` for changed Workboard and tooltip surfaces

target.title: feat(read): add encoding parameter for GBK and non-UTF-8 text files (#92664)
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes #92664. The built-in `read` session tool always decoded text files as UTF-8, which produced mojibake for files saved in legacy encodings such as GBK on Chinese Windows. This change adds an optional `encoding` parameter to the read tool and uses `iconv-lite` to decode the file buffer when a non-UTF-8 encoding is supplied. The OpenClaw read wrapper also strips malformed XML suffixes from the new `encoding` argument, matching the existing `path` cleanup.

## Changes
- `src/agents/sessions/tools/read.ts`: added `encoding` to the tool schema, wired it through `execute`, and added a `decodeReadBuffer` helper that falls back to UTF-8 when no encoding is provided.
- `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts`: included `encoding` in the malformed XML suffix cleanup alongside `path`.
- `src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts`: regression test verifying GBK decoding via injected operations.
- `scripts/repro/issue-92664-read-gbk.mts`: standalone reproduction script that exercises the real tool on a temporary GBK-encoded file.
- `package.json` / `pnpm-lock.yaml`: added `iconv-lite@0.7.2` as a direct dependency.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Reading a GBK-encoded text file now returns the correct Chinese characters instead of UTF-8 mojibake.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112, Node.js v22.19.0, local OpenClaw checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `pnpm test src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts --run`
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-92664-read-gbk.mts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```text
  PASS: GBK-encoded file decoded correctly.
  Decoded: 你好，世界
  Hello world
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The reproduction script and regression test both decode the GBK bytes to the original string.
- **What was not tested**: Reading the same file through the sandbox/host wrapper path (the wrapper only normalizes the encoding argument and delegates to the same tool), and reading GBK files on a real Windows filesystem.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts --run` — 4 passed
- `npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json src/agents/sessions/tools/read.ts src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts scripts/repro/issue-92664-read-gbk.mts` — clean
- `pnpm build` — passed

AI-assisted: changes were authored with Claude Code; the reproduction script and tests were run locally in this environment.

target.title: fix: recover stranded replies in message_tool_only mode (#85714)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.visibleReplies` is set to `message_tool`, the agent is expected to call the message tool to deliver replies. If the LLM forgets (a probabilistic event — the reporter saw it happen on the 8th turn after 7 correct deliveries), the reply was **silently discarded**: the substantive text sat in the JSONL transcript but was never delivered to the user.

The existing `shouldWarnAboutPrivateMessageToolFinal()` detection was already identifying these stranded replies and logging a warning, but took no recovery action.

## Fix

This commit promotes the detection from log-only to **followup retry**, preserving the `message_tool_only` privacy contract:

1. **Gate room events**: Skip stranded reply detection for ambient `room_event` turns where `message_tool_only` is automatically set by the system. Silence on room events is intentional by design — the agent chose not to respond.

2. **Enqueue followup retry turn**: When a stranded reply is detected, enqueue a followup run whose prompt instructs the agent to deliver the captured text via `message(action=send)`. The agent itself calls the message tool, so delivery happens through the correct contract path — no suppression bypass, no privacy boundary crossing.

3. **Clear transcript/persistence fields**: The retry run explicitly clears `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` from the original followup run to prevent the original user prompt from hiding the retry instruction. The followup runner resolves `transcriptPrompt ?? extracted.text`, so a retained `transcriptPrompt` would cause the agent to see the original user question instead of the retry delivery instruction.

4. **No suppression bypass**: This does NOT mark payloads with `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` or use `skipSuppressionCheck`. The `message_tool_only` privacy contract is fully preserved: delivery only happens when the agent explicitly calls `message(action=send)`.

Normal (non-stranded) paths are unaffected: short replies, intentional `NO_REPLY`, and runs where the message tool already delivered are all excluded by the existing `shouldWarnAboutPrivateMessageToolFinal` guard.

### Why followup retry instead of bypass delivery?

The `message_tool_only` contract says: "no message tool call = private/silent". Auto-publishing stranded text would break this documented behavior. Instead, we give the agent a second chance to follow the contract correctly — like returning an undelivered letter and asking the sender to mail it properly, rather than opening and publishing it ourselves.

## Changed files

- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` — followup retry with cleaned transcript fields, room_event gate (+26/-3 net)
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` — 5 new retry/gate tests replacing 6 bypass tests (-4 net)

## Tests

| Suite | Count | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| `agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts` | 47 | ✅ All pass |
| `private-message-tool-final.test.ts` | 8 | ✅ All pass |
| `dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | 189 | ✅ All pass (unchanged, confirms no regressions) |
| `followup-runner.test.ts` | 67 | ✅ All pass (confirms followup queue compatibility) |

New test cases:
- `enqueues a followup retry for a stranded substantive reply (#85714)` — verifies `enqueueFollowupRun` is called with retry prompt containing the assistant text
- `does not enqueue retry for short private final replies` — negative case
- `does not enqueue retry when the message tool delivered this turn` — negative case
- `does not enqueue retry for room_event turns (#85714)` — verifies room_event gate
- `clears transcriptPrompt on retry to prevent original prompt from hiding retry instruction (#85714)` — regression test verifying `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` are all `undefined` on the retry run

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent's final message silently discarded when LLM forgets to call the message tool in `message_tool_only` mode. The fix enqueues a followup retry turn so the agent can deliver via `message(action=send)`, preserving the privacy contract.
- **Real environment tested**: Local macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0, patched checkout at `9dc3c76dc3`.
- **Privacy contract preserved**: The fix does NOT use `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` or `skipSuppressionCheck`. No payload is auto-delivered. The agent is re-prompted to call `message(action=send)` itself.
- **Transcript isolation**: The retry run clears `transcriptPrompt`, `userTurnTranscriptRecorder`, and `currentInboundContext` so the followup runner's `transcriptPrompt ?? extracted.text` resolution correctly uses the retry prompt, not the original user message.
- **Room event safety**: Stranded reply detection is gated by `sessionCtx.InboundEventKind !== "room_event"`, so ambient room events (where silence is intentional) never trigger recovery. Covered by dedicated test case.
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main`, `warnPrivateMessageToolFinal` only logs — no retry is enqueued and the reply is silently lost.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  ✓ auto-reply agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts (47 tests) 1729ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  47 passed (47)

  ✓ unit-fast private-message-tool-final.test.ts (8 tests) 2ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  8 passed (8)

  ✓ auto-reply dispatch-from-config.test.ts (189 tests) 3770ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  189 passed (189)

  ✓ auto-reply followup-runner.test.ts (67 tests) 1710ms
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  67 passed (67)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: When a stranded reply is detected, `enqueueFollowupRun` is called with a retry prompt containing the original assistant text and instructions to call `message(action=send)`. The retry run has `transcriptPrompt: undefined` so the followup runner correctly uses the retry instruction. Short replies, already-delivered replies, room events, and runs with existing transcript prompts are all verified by dedicated test cases.
- **What was not tested**: Live end-to-end delivery through an actual channel — the test verifies that the correct followup run is enqueued with the expected prompt shape and cleaned fields. The followup queue machinery (`enqueueFollowupRun` → `scheduleFollowupDrain` → `runReplyAgent`) is a well-tested existing mechanism used for concurrent message queuing.

## Real behavior proof (end-to-end, qa-lab host runner)

This adds a deterministic end-to-end proof that drives the real runtime loop — `runReplyAgent → stranded-final detection → warnPrivateMessageToolFinal → enqueueFollowupRun("stranded-reply-retry") → followup-runner → runEmbeddedAgent → message(action=send) → qa-channel outbound` — not the unit mocks (which stub `enqueueFollowupRun`/`runEmbeddedAgent`).

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Under `messages.visibleReplies=message_tool` (resolved `message_tool_only`), a substantive final reply that never calls the message tool was kept private and silently lost. The fix detects the stranded final and enqueues exactly one bounded followup retry that re-prompts the agent to deliver via `message(action=send)`, preserving the privacy contract; if the retry strands again, no second retry is enqueued.
- **Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 24.3.0 arm64, Node.js v24.10.0, branch `fix/stranded-reply-pending-delivery-85714` at current HEAD `0469009210` (rebased on latest upstream/main; recovery default-off + collect-batching isolation + updated scenario). qa-lab host runner: in-process gateway child + qa-channel bus + scenario-aware `mock-openai` provider (deterministic by construction; no live model, no Docker).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran the new scenario, PASS:
  ```
  node scripts/run-node.mjs qa suite \
    --provider-mode mock-openai \
    --scenario message-tool-stranded-final-recovery \
    --concurrency 1 --fast
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: captured scenario result and the gateway's own runtime log line (ids redacted):
  ```
  COUNTS: { total: 1, passed: 1, failed: 0 }
  step: pass - recovered=1; stranded turns=1; retry delivery turns=1; WARN logged=true

  2026-06-06T06:31:23.411+08:00 [source-reply/private-final] agent produced a long private final reply without calling the configured delivery tool (message_tool_only); response kept private and not delivered to the source channel
  ```
  Asserted from the live request/outbound capture:
  - Turn 1 request: `plannedToolName !== "message"` (long plain-text final, stranded).
  - Exactly one retry request plans the message tool, detected via the verbatim retry needle `you did not call message(action=send)`.
  - Retry delivery request: `plannedToolName === "message"`, `plannedToolArgs.action === "send"`, `plannedToolArgs.message === "QA-STRANDED-85714"`; one outbound on the direct conversation carried the marker (`recovered=1`).
  - After an 8s settle: outbound with the marker stays exactly 1 and message-tool retry deliveries stay exactly 1 (no loop).
- **Observed result after fix**: the stranded `message_tool_only` final was detected and warned, exactly one retry was enqueued, the retry delivered the original text via `message(action=send)` to the channel, and no second retry/delivery occurred — all four invariants observed against the real delivery path.
- **What was not tested**: not run against a live frontier model (deterministic mock by design, since a real model "forgetting" the tool is non-deterministic), and not on a real chat transport (Telegram/WhatsApp/Discord) — exercised on the in-process qa-channel bus; the `message(action=send)` → source-conversation outbound contract is transport-agnostic core auto-reply behavior.

Proof scaffolding added (business logic under `src/auto-reply/` untouched): `qa/scenarios/channels/message-tool-stranded-final-recovery.md` (new scenario) and `extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.ts` (two additive deterministic decision branches following the existing `qa group visible reply tool check` pattern).

Closes #85714

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`**
- Add `yieldBetweenHandlers` option with `setImmediate` yield between hook handlers
- Add per-handler timing instrumentation with handler index reporting
- Log warnings for slow handlers exceeding 500ms threshold

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable `yieldBetweenHandlers` for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Support new async workspace file loading and timing callback interfaces

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — 3 new tests for yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning

All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509

## Summary

target.title: fix(status): avoid cumulative usage for context percent
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #83526.

Fix the TUI and `/status` Context line so explicitly stale cumulative session/cache totals cannot be used as current prompt occupancy, while legacy sessions with unknown `totalTokensFresh` still show their valid persisted total instead of `?`.

## Problem

The previous repair prevented stale cumulative totals from inflating Context, but it also required `totalTokensFresh === true` before any persisted `totalTokens` could render as Context. Legacy session entries often do not have that freshness flag, so valid unknown-freshness totals regressed to `Context: ?/...`.

## Root Cause

OpenClaw session metadata distinguishes three states:

- `totalTokensFresh: true`: known current/fresh prompt usage.
- `totalTokensFresh: false`: explicitly stale cumulative usage.
- `totalTokensFresh` missing: legacy/unknown freshness, not automatically stale.

The status builder treated the missing flag like stale data, even though the session helper contract only rejects explicit `false`.

## Solution

- Restore `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens` as the canonical persisted-total gate: explicit `false` is stale; missing freshness is legacy/unknown and remains usable.
- Let a fresh transcript prompt snapshot replace legacy/unknown persisted totals when available, so older cumulative totals do not override better current-context evidence.
- Preserve explicit-fresh persisted totals unless the transcript candidate is larger.
- Add regression coverage for legacy unknown-freshness totals rendering as Context instead of `?`.
- Clarify the status comment around legacy unknown freshness versus explicit stale totals.

## Dependency Contract Proof

- `../codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/protocol.rs:1964` defines `TokenUsage`, and `../codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/protocol.rs:1977` exposes both `total_token_usage` and `last_token_usage` in `TokenUsageInfo`.
- `../codex/codex-rs/protocol/src/protocol.rs:1987` shows `append_last_usage` accumulates total usage separately from the last-turn/current usage.
- `../codex/codex-rs/core/src/session/mod.rs:1171` documents the separate `token_usage_info` path for restored usage, distinct from plain cumulative totals.
- `../codex/codex-rs/core/src/context_manager/history.rs:283` starts context accounting from the most recent model-emitted usage, matching the need to avoid treating cumulative cache/session totals as current prompt occupancy.

## Testing Performed

- `cmd.exe /C pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/status/status-message.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts`
  - Passed.
- `cmd.exe /C git diff --check -- src/status/status-message.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts`
  - Passed.
- `cmd.exe /C node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts`
  - Passed: 82 tests.
- `cmd.exe /C node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts --testNamePattern "Codex OAuth context|stale persisted"`
  - Passed: 2 tests, 25 skipped by pattern.
- `cmd.exe /C "set PYTHONUTF8=1&&python .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD"`
  - Passed: autoreview clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Release Note

Fixes `/status` and TUI Context reporting for legacy sessions so valid unknown-freshness totals no longer render as unknown, while explicitly stale cumulative totals remain excluded from current context occupancy.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/status` and TUI Context reporting no longer treats missing `totalTokensFresh` as stale, while explicit stale cumulative totals still cannot become the Context numerator.

Real environment tested: Local Windows/WSL OpenClaw source checkout at commit `8ca9ac38ef`, running the patched OpenClaw status formatter through Windows Node with `tsx`; no credentials or live user data were used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary local driver that imported `buildStatusMessage` from the patched checkout, seeded a redacted legacy session entry with `totalTokens: 25_000`, `contextTokens: 1_000_000`, and no `totalTokensFresh`, then ran `cmd.exe /C node --import tsx .tmp-openclaw-status-proof.ts` and removed the temporary driver.

Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
OpenClaw /status formatter live output (redacted local source checkout)
scenario=legacy-session-totalTokensFresh-omitted totalTokens=25000 contextTokens=1000000
🦞 OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (8ca9ac3)
🧠 Model: anthropic/test:opus · 🔑 api-key
📚 Context: 25k/1.0m (3%) · 🧹 Compactions: 0
🧵 Session: agent:main:main • updated 10m ago
⚙️ Execution: direct · Runtime: OpenClaw Default · Think: off · Fast: off · elevated
🪢 Queue: collect (depth 0)
```

Observed result after fix: The redacted live output shows the legacy unknown-freshness session renders a concrete `Context: 25k/1.0m (3%)` value instead of `Context: ?/1.0m`, while the supplemental regression tests still cover explicit stale totals and fresh transcript replacement.

What was not tested: A live TUI or channel session reproduction, a coverage report, and the full local `commands-status.test.ts` file; the whole-file local run stayed quiet for several minutes and was stopped, so full-lane proof is left to CI on this pushed SHA.

target.title: fix(cron): persist resolved delivery target into isolated cron session deliveryContext
target.body:
## Summary

Isolated cron completion delivery can fail with `Channel is required (no configured channels detected)` even when the job explicitly sets `delivery.channel`, because the resolved delivery target is never written back to the isolated cron session entry's `deliveryContext`. Completion-announce paths such as `sessions_send`/`subagent_announce` later resolve the target from the session entry and see an empty context.

This change persists the resolved cron delivery target onto the isolated cron session entry right after `resolveCronDeliveryContext`, so explicit `delivery.channel` survives to the final completion announcer.

Fixes #92460.

## Changes

- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-session-state.ts`: add `setCronSessionDeliveryContextFromResolvedDelivery`, a focused helper that normalizes and writes a resolved delivery target into the session entry's `deliveryContext` only when resolution succeeds and a routable target exists.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`: call the helper in `prepareCronRunContext` after `resolveCronDeliveryContext` returns.
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-session-state.test.ts`: add regression tests covering explicit target persistence, missing optional fields, failed-resolution no-op, and empty-target no-op.
- `scripts/repro/issue-92460-cron-completion-delivery-context.mts`: standalone reproduction script that exercises the production helper and verifies the explicit channel/to/account/thread survive.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: explicit `delivery.channel` on an isolated cron job is now persisted onto the isolated session entry, so completion-announce target resolution no longer falls back to a channel-less main session.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node v22.19.0, local OpenClaw source checkout.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-92460-cron-completion-delivery-context.mts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  Terminal output from running the standalone reproduction script against the patched source:
  ```text
  === Reproduction for issue #92460 ===
  Isolated cron session deliveryContext: {
    \"channel\": \"webchat\",
    \"to\": \"controller\",
    \"accountId\": \"default\",
    \"threadId\": \"thread-42\"
  }
  PASS: explicit delivery.channel survives to the session entry deliveryContext
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: the isolated cron session entry now carries a normalized deliveryContext with the explicit channel, to, account, and thread from the resolved delivery target. Before the patch the same script would leave deliveryContext empty and the entry would lose the explicit webchat target.
- **What was not tested**: live channel send integration (no configured webchat gateway in the test environment); only the session-state propagation path and existing cron delivery-target resolution tests were exercised.

## Verification

- [x] `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-92460-cron-completion-delivery-context.mts` passes
- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run-session-state.test.ts --run` passes
- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-target.test.ts --run` passes
- [x] `pnpm test src/cron/isolated-agent/run.session-key-isolation.test.ts --run` passes
- [x] `npx oxlint` on changed files passes
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt` on changed files passes
- [x] `pnpm build` passes
- [x] `pnpm tsgo` passes

target.title: feat(browser): extend --labels overlay to full-page and element captures
target.body: Bring extensions/browser `--labels` screenshot to its full intended scope: full-page captures, element/ref captures, and a per-ref bounding-box metadata array — while preserving the shipped `labelsSkipped` response contract. - `--labels --full-page` now actually projects labels across the entire page (previously silently dropped fullPage in the Playwright path). - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` now clip the capture and project labels relative to the element top-left (previously ref/element were silently dropped in the labels path). - Response includes a new `annotations` array of per-ref bounding boxes (`{ref, number, role, name?, box: {x,y,width,height}}`) so agents can reason about positions without re-snapshotting. The field is omitted when empty. - `labelsSkipped` keeps its shipped meaning: in viewport capture, refs outside the captured viewport are counted as skipped (not silently re-interpreted). `labelsCount` reports the labels actually drawn on the image; `annotations` is an additive superset that also includes off-viewport refs with their boxes. Implementation: - New `screenshot-annotate.ts` pure helper: `refToNumber`, `planAnnotations` with viewport/fullpage/element coordinate projection (with overlap filter for element mode), `buildOverlayInjectionScript` and `buildOverlayClearScript` IIFE generators. Zero Playwright dependency so the helper is reusable for the raw-CDP path in a follow-up. - `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` rewritten to consume the helper. It reads scroll + viewport size in one `page.evaluate`; in viewport capture mode, refs whose document box falls outside the viewport rect are counted as skipped and surfaced in `annotations` but not drawn (and do not consume the label budget). `labels` returns the drawn overlay count. - `/screenshot` Playwright branch forwards fullPage/ref/element through to the helper and returns annotations. - `/snapshot` Playwright branch returns annotations only (snapshot remains intentionally viewport-only and does not gain new params). - LLM-facing safeDetails surfaces annotations to the agent. Behavior summary: - `labelsSkipped` (viewport): preserved — off-viewport refs raise the count, same as shipped `main`. `labelsCount` (viewport): drawn-in-viewport labels (matches shipped `main`). New `annotations` array is additive. - Existing fullPage + (ref|element) 400 validation is unchanged. chrome-mcp and raw-CDP paths are deliberately unchanged in this change. existing-session profiles continue to omit the `annotations` field. That is documented as a known downgrade and tracked for follow-up. Tests: - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` (31 cases): `refToNumber` forms; three coordinate-space projections; element-mode overlap filter; maxLabels truncation; empty-name omission; IIFE script content/idempotency; and viewport off-screen accounting (off-viewport counted as skipped, still surfaced in annotations, only when viewport size is supplied). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` (7 cases): all three capture modes, finally-cleanup-on-error, off-viewport-counted-as-skipped, and bbox-failure accounting. - All existing extensions/browser tests still pass. ## Review follow-ups (ClawSweeper) This revision addresses both findings from the review: - **[P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics** — Resolved by preserving the shipped contract instead of narrowing it. In viewport capture, off-viewport refs are counted as skipped again (matching `main`); `labelsCount` reports drawn-in-viewport labels; the new `annotations` array is purely additive (it includes off-viewport refs with their boxes so agents can still locate them). No existing consumer of `labelsSkipped` is silently re-interpreted, so no maintainer compatibility decision is required. - **[P2] raw-CDP labels caveat** — Resolved. The browser-automation skill now states that the raw-CDP fallback (no Playwright) does not support labeled screenshots at all (returns 501); only chrome-mcp/existing-session renders the overlay without `annotations`. - **[P1] driver-scoped label docs** — Resolved. - **[P1] scoped capture compatibility** — Contributor chooses Accept scoped-label behavior; see Compatibility decision. No opt-in API. `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and the browser automation skill now scope full-page/ref/element label projection and `annotations` to Playwright-backed profiles, describe existing-session/chrome-mcp as overlay-only without `annotations`, and keep raw-CDP documented as labels-unsupported without Playwright. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? The Playwright path of `extensions/browser` `--labels` screenshot silently dropped three caller-supplied parameters: `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element`. A user passing `--labels --full-page` got a viewport-only capture with no error. A user passing `--labels --ref e3` got a viewport capture of the whole page, ignoring the element scoping. The labels response also gave the LLM no way to reason about a ref's on-image position without re-running snapshot, so multimodal agents had to re-snapshot for every layout question. Why does this matter now? The `--labels` flag is the workhorse for vision-capable agents working on long pages or single components. The silent-drop made any prompt-engineered combination such as "screenshot the form with labels" produce wrong-but-not-erroring output, which is a worse failure mode than a clear 4xx. Adding the `annotations` array is the single biggest information-density gain for that prompt path: positions become a few hundred bytes of structured JSON instead of a re-issued snapshot RPC. What is the intended outcome? - `--labels --full-page` produces a full-page PNG/JPG with labels in document coordinates, all the way to the bottom of the page. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` produce an element-clip PNG with labels projected relative to the element top-left, only including refs that overlap the element rect. - Every Playwright-path labels response includes `annotations: [{ref, number, role, name?, box}]` (omitted when empty). - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is a new additive field. - A pure helper (`screenshot-annotate.ts`) holds all coordinate math and DOM-injection IIFE strings, so the same logic can be reused by the raw-CDP path later without refactor. What is intentionally out of scope? - chrome-mcp path. `existing-session` profiles continue to use `renderChromeMcpLabels` and continue to omit the `annotations` field; this is a documented downgrade. - Raw-CDP fallback. Labels require Playwright; without it the route returns 501 (was already the case). - The `snapshot` route does not gain `fullPage / ref / element` parameters; the accompanying screenshot stays viewport-only. - No new CLI flag. `--labels` keeps its current shape; only the help text and the response payload were updated. - No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING.md). What does success look like? - Full page (`https://playwright.dev/`, ~4121px tall) with `--labels --full-page`: every interactive ref is drawn and annotated in document coordinates, `labelsSkipped: 0`, and after capture `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length === 0` (no DOM residue). - Same page with viewport `--labels`: only refs inside the viewport are drawn; refs below the fold are counted in `labelsSkipped` (preserving the shipped contract) and still listed in `annotations` so an agent can scroll to them. What should reviewers focus on? - Coordinate-space correctness: `planAnnotations` projects `box` relative to viewport scroll for `viewport`, returns doc-space for `fullpage`, and projects relative to `elementRect` (with an overlap filter) for `element`. Tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock each axis. - `labelsSkipped` preservation: in viewport mode an off-viewport ref raises `labelsSkipped`, is not drawn, and is still present in `annotations`. `labelsCount` counts drawn labels. `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` stays disjoint and equals the total resolved refs, matching shipped `main`. - Cleanup invariant: `buildOverlayClearScript()` MUST run even when `page.screenshot()` throws. Covered by `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` with a screenshot mock that throws. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (No tracking issue exists. The behavior gaps were discovered while building a multimodal browser agent prompt and the `--labels` family is the natural seam for the fix.) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # (None I am aware of. If a maintainer points to one I will link it.) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No. Drive-by contribution. The change is self-contained: deleting the new helper file and reverting `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` removes the feature cleanly. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: - `--labels --full-page` was producing viewport-only output (silently dropping `fullPage`). - `--labels --ref ` / `--labels --element ` were producing whole-page viewport output (silently dropping the scope). - `--labels` responses had no per-ref bounding box data; agents had to re-snapshot to learn positions. - Review follow-up: `labelsSkipped` must keep its shipped meaning (off-viewport refs counted as skipped) rather than being narrowed. - Real environment tested: - OS: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 - Node: v24.16.0 - Browser: headless Chromium (Playwright-bundled), driver `openclaw`, `cdpPort 9222` - Profile: `--profile dev` running `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run` (source mode, freshly-built code paths) - Target page: `https://playwright.dev/` (~4121px tall scrollable document) - Head: `20c9435b5ea024c62cf38db638898787a0e1ea99` (current HEAD; all evidence below was re-captured against this SHA after rebasing on latest `main`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ``` alias oc='node openclaw.mjs --profile dev' oc browser open https://playwright.dev --label pa # Case 1: snapshot with annotations (viewport-only accompanying screenshot) oc browser --json snapshot --target-id pa --interactive --labels # Case 2: viewport screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels # Case 3: full-page screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --full-page # Case 4: element-clip screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --element "nav" ``` - Evidence after fix (live JSON summaries, copied from the runs at HEAD `88942b1097`): | # | Command | labelsCount | labelsSkipped | annotations | max box.y | |---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:| | 1 | snapshot --interactive --labels (viewport) | 11 | 32 | 43 | 4121 | | 2 | screenshot --labels (viewport) | 13 | 51 | 64 | 4121 | | 3 | screenshot --labels --full-page | 64 | 0 | 64 | 1928 | | 4 | screenshot --labels --element "nav" | 4 | 0 | 4 | 15 | Reading of the numbers: - **`labelsSkipped` is preserved**: viewport captures (rows 1, 2) report 32 and 51 off-viewport refs as skipped — exactly the shipped behavior the review asked to keep. The pre-fix revision of this PR reported `labelsSkipped: 0` here. - **`annotations` is additive**: rows 1, 2 still surface all 43 / 64 resolved refs (including off-viewport ones, whose `box.y` reaches 4121 — outside the viewport image), so an agent can locate and scroll to them. - **Disjoint and consistent**: `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` equals the resolved-ref total in every row (11+32=43, 13+51=64, 64+0=64, 4+0=4). - **Full-page (row 3)**: the whole document is captured, so nothing is off-viewport: `labelsSkipped: 0`, all 64 drawn, and `max(box.y)=1928 ≤ resized image height (~2000)`. Visual captures from `https://playwright.dev/` (overlay/clipping behavior, unchanged by the `labelsSkipped` fix): **Viewport mode** (`screenshot --labels`): **Full-page mode** (`screenshot --labels --full-page`) — labels span the entire scrollable document: **Element-clip mode** (`screenshot --labels --element/--ref`) — capture clipped to the element rect; labels projected relative to the element top-left: - Observed result after fix: - `--labels --full-page` now returns a full-page labeled screenshot instead of silently falling back to the viewport-only capture. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` now return element-clipped labeled screenshots with label boxes projected relative to the clipped image. - Viewport labeled captures preserve the shipped `labelsSkipped` behavior: off-viewport refs are counted as skipped, while `annotations` still lists the resolved refs and boxes for agent positioning. - The live JSON summaries above show the intended after-fix counts for viewport, snapshot, full-page, and element-clip modes. - What was not tested: - chrome-mcp path on `existing-session` profile. Code review confirms the labels branch routes to `renderChromeMcpLabels` first; `annotations` is intentionally absent there. - Raw-CDP fallback. Code review confirms `--labels` requires Playwright and returns 501 otherwise. - DOM cleanup was not re-captured live this round (the `evaluate` CLI rejects a label-form `--target-id` in this flow); the `finally`-clear invariant is locked by the `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` "runs the clear script even when screenshot throws" case. - macOS / Windows. Linux only; no OS-specific code paths touched. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Headless mode only. Headed-mode behavior should be identical (same `page.screenshot()` / `locator.screenshot()` calls). - Before evidence: - Before this fix on `main`, `--labels --full-page` produced a viewport-only PNG with no labels below the fold, and `--labels --ref/--element` ignored the scope. The response had no `annotations` field. The pre-fix revision of this PR additionally reported `labelsSkipped: 0` for viewport captures (the narrowing this revision reverts). ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot-annotate.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/client.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts pnpm tsgo:extensions pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main ``` All green. The two annotate suites report `38 passed`; the labels-touching route/tool suites report `92 passed`. `check:changed` (typecheck extensions + extension tests + extension lint + guards) passes against latest `main`. What regression coverage was added or updated? - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts`: added a "viewport off-screen accounting" group — off-viewport refs are counted as skipped and still surfaced in `annotations` when viewport size is supplied, and not counted when it is omitted (pure-helper default). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts`: the former "off-viewport not filtered" case is now "off-viewport counted as skipped but still surfaced in annotations" (asserts `skipped`, `labels`, and `annotations`). ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, additive and compatibility-preserving: - `--labels --full-page` now produces full-page output (was silently viewport-only). - `--labels --ref`/`--element` now produce element-clip output (was silently viewport-only). - New `annotations` array on Playwright-path labels responses. - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures (off-viewport refs counted as skipped; drawn count reported), so existing consumers are not silently re-interpreted. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No config schema changes, no migrations, no new env vars, no plugin SDK surface changes. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No new permissions, no SSRF policy changes, no new network destinations, no secret-surface changes, no `evaluate`/`act` changes. What is the highest-risk area? Coordinate-space invariants in `planAnnotations` (viewport vs fullpage vs element). A bug means agents reason about the wrong screen pixel. Mitigated by the three projection tests plus the live proof above. Secondary risk — DOM residue if cleanup fails — is mitigated by the unconditional `finally { clearScript }` block and the cleanup-on-error test. ## Compatibility decision (ClawSweeper P1) Contributor position: **Accept scoped-label behavior** (ClawSweeper maintainer option 1). No opt-in `labelsScope` / `labelsScoped` flag. **Shipped behavior (main):** On the Playwright labeled screenshot path, `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element` were not forwarded to `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright`. Combinations such as `--labels --full-page` returned HTTP 200 with a **viewport-only** labeled capture while silently ignoring the scope flags. The unlabeled screenshot path already honored those flags. **New behavior (this PR):** Labeled Playwright screenshots honor caller-supplied `fullPage` / `ref` / `element`, matching the unlabeled path, CLI help, and the intended `--labels` contract. **Breaking surface:** Callers that passed `labels` together with `fullPage` / `ref` / `element` and relied on the old viewport-only labeled output. This is narrower than a general screenshot API break: chrome-mcp, raw-CDP, and `snapshot --labels` scope are unchanged. **Why acceptable:** The prior behavior was a silent bug (wrong-but-not-erroring output). Honoring scope aligns labeled and unlabeled paths and matches documented CLI semantics. `labelsSkipped` / `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is additive. **Mitigations in this PR:** - `labelsSkipped` semantics preserved (commit `88942b1`). - Driver differences documented in `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and the browser automation skill. - Docs note that prior releases ignored scope on labeled Playwright screenshots (commit `771af62c70`). - P3 docs indent fixed (`docs/cli/browser.md` annotation schema line). **Request to maintainers:** Please confirm acceptance of scoped-label behavior as an intended bug fix so this PR can land without a compatibility opt-in shim. | Scenario | main | this PR | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `screenshot --labels` | viewport + off-screen skipped | same + `annotations` | compatible | | `screenshot --labels --full-page` | viewport (scope ignored) | full-page | intentional fix | | `screenshot --labels --element nav` | viewport (scope ignored) | element clip | intentional fix | | `snapshot --labels` | viewport | viewport + `annotations` | compatible | ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review and explicit acceptance of scoped-label behavior (Compatibility decision). Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? - ClawSweeper [P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics: addressed by preserving the shipped contract (see Review follow-ups + Real behavior proof rows 1-3). - ClawSweeper [P2] raw-CDP labels caveat: addressed in `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`. - ClawSweeper [P1] driver-scoped label docs: addressed across `docs/cli/browser.md`, `docs/tools/browser-control.md`, and `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`. - ClawSweeper [P1] scoped capture compatibility: contributor documents Accept scoped-label behavior; awaiting maintainer confirmation. - ClawSweeper [P3] docs annotation schema indent: fixed in `771af62c70`.
